dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Anne. The village is falling apart. I need to know what's really going on.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Took you long enough to ask. Sarah's been holed up in the laboratory working on a cure. Last anyone heard, she'd made progress.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: And now?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Now she's gone quiet. The lab's locked tight and the power's out. Word is the key's buried out at the graveyard — old caretaker kept it on him.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: And the power?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Talk to George at the powerstation. He'll know what the lab needs. And be careful — Viktor's people are watching the roads.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: George. I need power back at the laboratory.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Lab's on its own circuit. Needs a fuse. There was a spare inside the lab itself — last I checked.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Anything else?

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Bring the fuse here. I'll talk you through the install. Grid's stable otherwise.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Understood.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Sarah? You're... you're infected.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: I know. Pricked myself with a contaminated sample three days ago. Stupid mistake.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Can you still work?

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Not for much longer. The synthesis machine is ready — it just needs power. There's a fuse on the bench. Take it. Then bring me a fresh cure sample from the hospital. After that... synthesize. The machine will do most of the work.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: And then I use the antidote on you.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Then you use it on me. Hurry. I can feel it spreading.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Marcus. I need the cure sample stored here. Sarah's infected — it's her only chance.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: Sample's on the shelf. Take it. I'm holding this position regardless.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Appreciate it.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: Don't waste it. That's the only one I've seen come through.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: It worked. The machine produced an antidote.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Good. Inject me — quickly. Then we have work to do. One antidote isn't enough. The village needs more.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Hold still.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Sarah, this outbreak — it's not natural, is it? The samples I've seen, the way it spreads. Someone made this.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: I've been arriving at the same conclusion. The pathogen is engineered. Whoever designed it understood viral architecture at a level that takes resources. Real resources.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Resources like a private lab. A funded operation.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: There's a man at the outpost. They call him the Doctor. I've intercepted enough fragments of his notes to believe he's the architect. Viktor protects him — gives him a controlled environment and people to test on.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Then we need to get into the outpost. End this at the source.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: The outpost is locked. There's a key — Anne at the store hears everything that passes through the village. Start there. She'll know where it ended up.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: And if I find the Doctor?

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Get me anything you can from his work. If we can confirm what he's done, we can undo it. But be careful. Viktor will not let him be taken quietly.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Anne. I need to know about the outpost. The locked one.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: That's a particular kind of question. The kind that gets people killed if the wrong ears hear it.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Sarah thinks the man inside is responsible for the outbreak. The Doctor.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: I've thought as much for weeks. People go up that road and don't come back the same — if they come back at all.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Is there a key?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Viktor keeps the spares close, but one went missing months ago. A scavenger took it and hid it in the cave east of the village before he turned. I'd start there.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Thank you, Anne.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Don't thank me yet. Whatever's in that outpost, it wants to stay hidden. Be quick, and don't come back through here bleeding.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: George. I'm going into the outpost. Anything I should know?

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: It draws power off a private line. Not mine. Viktor had it run in before the outbreak.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Off the grid entirely?

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Off mine. Means whatever they're running in there, they don't want me near it. That tells you enough.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: It does. Thanks.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Mind the patrol road. Viktor walks it.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I have the key.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Good. Then I'll give you something else — a name carries weight when you confront a man like that. He calls himself the Doctor. He won't fight you. He'll talk. That's worse.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Worse how?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: He'll try to convince you that what he's done was necessary. Don't let him finish the sentence. Get what you need and get out.

dialogue_content.speaker: viktor
dialogue_content.line: You walked in through my front door. That takes nerve. Or stupidity. I haven't decided which.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I'm here for the Doctor. Step aside.

dialogue_content.speaker: viktor
dialogue_content.line: The Doctor is the only reason this island still has a future worth fighting over. You want to take him from me? You'd better understand what you're walking into.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: He engineered the outbreak. People are dying because of him.

dialogue_content.speaker: viktor
dialogue_content.line: People were always going to die. He's the one who decides what comes after. I'd kill you where you stand — but I'm curious. Let's see what he makes of you.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: You'll regret letting me past.

dialogue_content.speaker: viktor
dialogue_content.line: We'll see. He's through the next door. Go on.

dialogue_content.speaker: the_doctor
dialogue_content.line: Ah. The variable I've been hearing about. Sit, if you like. Or don't. It changes very little.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: You did this. The outbreak. The village. All of it.

dialogue_content.speaker: the_doctor
dialogue_content.line: I designed a vector. The outbreak was an inevitability of human contact patterns. I merely accelerated what was already coming.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: People are dead. Children. You call that an inevitability?

dialogue_content.speaker: the_doctor
dialogue_content.line: I call it data. And data, when properly collected, is the only path to a cure. Sarah is trying to reverse-engineer my work. She won't succeed without my notes.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Then I'll take them.

dialogue_content.speaker: the_doctor
dialogue_content.line: You may. I have backups you cannot reach. But take the sample on the bench — it represents months of refinement. Sarah will need it. Consider it... a professional courtesy.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I'm not leaving you here to keep working.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I made it into the outpost. Found the Doctor. He confirmed everything — he engineered the pathogen.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: And he let you walk out?

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: He thinks his work is bigger than any one room. I brought back a toolkit from his workshop. It's the only useful thing I could carry without him knowing what it meant.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: A toolkit may be more useful than you realise. Mary's been waiting on one for the boat. If we lose this fight, we'll need a way off the island for the people who are still healthy.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: And the cure?

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: I'll keep working. I have enough of his methodology now to know where to look. But get that toolkit to Mary — we may need that boat sooner than either of us would like.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Understood.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: And next time you face the Doctor — don't talk to him. Just finish it.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: You've got the look of someone bringing news. Make it quick.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I have a toolkit. Sarah thought you might need it more than she does.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Sarah's got sense. That's half of what I need to get her running again. Hand it here.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Take it. We may need that boat before the week's out.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Then I'd best stop talking and start working. You find me wood, and we'll have a way out for anyone with the sense to take it.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Mary. I heard there might still be a way off this island.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: There is. She's right behind me. Problem is she's got more holes than hull right now.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: What do you need?

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Wood. Good wood, not driftwood. And I'll need my toolkit back in my own hands when you bring the wood — or yours, doesn't matter. Bring me what I need and I'll have her ready by sundown.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Anything else?

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Yeah. Anyone you want out of here, get them down to this dock. Once she floats, I'm not waiting.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Anne. Mary's getting the boat seaworthy. I need to gather people.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: About time someone started thinking that way. Sarah's still at the lab — she'd want to know. So would Marcus, if you can pry him off that hospital floor.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: And Linda?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Linda will say no. She always says no. But she's got food on that farm and you'll want it for the crossing. Be polite. She earns her quiet.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Thanks, Anne. You coming with us?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Get the others moving first. We'll talk when the boat actually floats.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Wood. Toolkit. Let's fix your boat.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Now you're talking. Patch the starboard side first — that's where she's drinking water.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: On it.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Linda. There's a boat at the dock. It's leaving tonight.

dialogue_content.speaker: linda
dialogue_content.line: Figured something was stirring. Birds went quiet this morning.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: We'll need food for the crossing. Yours is the only farm still producing.

dialogue_content.speaker: linda
dialogue_content.line: Take what's in the crate by the porch. I've kept enough back for myself.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: You're not coming?

dialogue_content.speaker: linda
dialogue_content.line: My land's here. Always has been. You go on. Tell whoever's captaining that boat I said safe seas.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Sarah. Mary's boat is being repaired. We can leave tonight.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Leave. After everything we've worked toward.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Bring your research. The work continues somewhere it can actually save people.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: ...That's a reasonable point. Off-island labs still exist. Continuity matters more than location. I'll pack what I can carry and meet you at the dock.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Marcus is at the hospital. I'm going for him next.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Good. He won't move on his own. He never does.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Marcus. We're leaving the island. Boat's at the dock.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: I've got a position here. Civilians depend on this floor staying held.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: The civilians are coming with us. Sarah. Anne. Mary. The position is the boat now.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: ...Acceptable. A position you can defend at sea is better than one you can't resupply on land.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Get to the dock. Bring whatever you can carry.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: Moving. Don't leave without me.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Food's loaded. Sarah and Marcus are on their way. How's the boat?

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: She's tight on the starboard side but the port hull's still weeping. One more patch and she'll hold all the way to the mainland.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Let's finish her.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Then we ride the tide out. No one stops us, not Viktor, not his doctor, no one.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: That's her. She'll hold.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Then cast off. We're done here.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Aye. Get on board. The island's behind us from here.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Goodbye, Linda. Goodbye, Anne. Hope you outlast it.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Ropes are off. Hold on — here we go.
